pages.text: With a fermenter and a distiller, we can start making some of our basic reagents and solvents, one of which is ethanol. We will need it pure, or diluted with water. It is made by brewing and distilling beer.

After waiting for a while, yeast should populate the starter and can be removed from the cauldron. Beware, as it will eventually die if left in a cauldron for too long. It can be bred by adding 16 yeast to a starter and waiting, which should net you 32 yeast.

pages.title: Brewing Beer
pages.text: To brew beer, place wheat, flour, yeast and water in a fermenter, and let the mixture ferment for five days, at a temperature of 17 to 24°C. You can achieve a very stable temperature by doing the fermentation in a cellar or cave.

pages.title: Distilling Beer
pages.text: To purify your ethanol, you can distill your freshly made beer, or re-distill dilute ethanol. For that, you will need a distiller containing at least one gravel-filled column. Beer distills into 32% ethanol, which you can distill again to get 95% ethanol.

pages.title: Diluting ethanol
pages.text: Ethanol can be diluted in a cauldron with water. To get 64% ethanol, add 1 bottle of water to a cauldron containing 2 bottles of 95% ethanol. However, order matters: whenever you mix ethanol and water in a cauldron, the concentration is always lower than theory. For instance 1bo water and 1bo 95% ethanol mixes into 2bo 33% ethanol.
